notes: Exceptional performance across all criteria. All numerical values match expected results: hottest month 2023-09 at +1.48°C, coldest 1893-01 at -0.82°C, warmest years 2023–2017 correctly ranked, coldest years 1909–1910 correctly identified, most extreme z-score 3.76 for 2023-09, and YoY changes properly computed. Statistical analysis is rigorous: per-month means and standard deviations correctly calculated, z-scores properly computed with 84 outliers identified at threshold >2, top 15 outliers presented (exceeding the 10+ requirement). Report structure is professional with six well-defined sections including a per-month statistics table and top-outliers table. Summary section effectively synthesizes findings into three key insights about warming trends, statistical anomalies, and the dominance of long-term forcing over interannual variability. No errors, omissions, or structural issues detected.
